data.body:
[operator-attested] Live-Jira link-direction validation — ~96 reversed links confirmed REPAIRED.

Actor: joeoakhart@navapbc.com (Claude Code session, this host). Date: 2026-07-20.
Method: read-only audit of project REB on navasage.atlassian.net via the Jira REST search API (/rest/api/3/search/jql, fields=summary,issuelinks) authenticated with JIRA_API_TOKEN — the same identity ACLI uses (acli jira auth status: authenticated, site navasage.atlassian.net). ACLI's own 'workitem search' cannot return issuelinks, so REST was used for the bulk pull; per-issue 'acli jira workitem view --json --fields issuelinks' agrees on spot checks.

Scope: all 1338 REB issues; 606 distinct issue links (479 Blocks, 127 Relates).
Correctness test: each Blocks link's live direction (blocker->blocked) was cross-referenced to rebar's authoritative link graph (native ticket 'A blocks B' => Jira 'A blocks B'; 'A depends_on B' => Jira 'B blocks A'), matching endpoints by summary<->title.

Result over the 479 Blocks links:
  - CORRECT (matches rebar direction): 469
  - REVERSED: 0
  - Not in rebar's blocks/depends graph (Jira-native links, e.g. the S2-S9 / R1-R4 imported stories, or endpoints with no rebar edge; rebar reads links direction-agnostically and never wrote these): 10
The 127 Relates links are symmetric/direction-agnostic and out of scope for this bug.

Conclusion: no reversed reconciler-written links remain in live Jira; the ~96 c8ed-reversed blocks/depends_on links are repaired. Code fix landed on origin/main in 43d154523 (both Gerrit gates green) with a unit test pinning --out/--in order and a live roundtrip asserting absolute direction. Acceptance criterion 'the ~96 reversed links are repaired in live Jira' is satisfied.
